page: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. This x86_64 architecture platform features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, though it does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The platform excels in memory-intensive tasks, demonstrating top-tier read and write memory bandwidth alongside exceptional database operations performance, as evidenced by 533.75 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), drive top-tier results in cryptographic operations, software compilation, and ray tracing.

While the server delivers strong prompt processing speeds for large language models, its text generation speeds are average to poor due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. This bare-metal configuration is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive CPU density and memory bandwidth without the overhead of a hypervisor. It is optimally suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput static web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and intensive enterprise-grade general-purpose computing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPUs. The platform delivers top-tier multi-core compute and memory bandwidth, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Redis SET operations. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, it excels in cryptography, software compilation, and database operations. However, text generation for LLM inference remains a bottleneck due to the absence of GPU acceleration. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and heavy multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core compute and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform delivering exceptional multi-core compute, high-speed DDR5 memory bandwidth, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores.
Equipped with 1536 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated cryptography and matrix math.
Optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and heavy multi-threaded workloads.
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, limiting its efficiency for LLM text generation.
